revision.text:
[[ja:速川神社 (西都市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hayakawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hayakawa shrine.JPG |location = Minamikata Torisu 183-2, Saito City, Miyazaki Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.16225|N|131.348306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Seoritsu-hime
Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto
Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto
Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto |type = |consecration_year = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Co11er Roof |Other names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = December 12 |Rituals = Summer Festival (June 12)
Midwinter Purification (January) |location = {{ill|Saito|qid=Q850479|1=Saito, Miyazaki}} }} '''Hayakawa Shrine''' (速川神社, Hayakawa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan. == Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|lt=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|速開津比売|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|ja|気吹戸主神|lt=Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi no Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} == History == The founding date of the shrine is unknown. It is believed to have been a place of worship on the left bank of the Hayakawa rapids. The shrine's location is depicted in the ''Jindai Kōto Ezu'' written by Kodama Sanemitsu, a village headman of Miyake Village (modern Saito City) in Koyu District, born in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era|1=Meiwa}} 1 (1764). The shrine was relocated to its current site in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 14 (1925) and was officially recognized as a religious corporation in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 46 (1971). == Shrine Buildings == The main hall is built in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, and the worship hall follows the {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} style, both with co11er roofs. == Access == The shrine is accessible by taking a Miyazaki Kōtsū Bus from Saito Bus Center towards Murasho. Alight at the Hayakawa Shrine-mae bus stop and walk for approximately 20 minutes. == References == * ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Journal,'' Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Office, Shōwa 63 (1988). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|速川神社 (西都市)|version=100477326|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:速川神社 (西都市)]] [[ja:速川神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11639672}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
